codeReview.summary: The feature substantially improves Lanes polish, cached-state messaging, and live-action gating, but it also regresses the benchmark lane-detail flow by treating diff inspection as a live-only action. That breaks the contract that cached lane detail stays useful while only mutating git actions disable during disconnect/sync states.

issues.description: The new live-action gate now disables the per-file `Diff` button whenever `allowsLiveActions` is false (`LaneFileTreeComponents.swift:306-307`), and recent-commit diff entry is gated the same way via the history `Files` button (`apps/ios/ADE/Views/Lanes/LaneDetailGitSection.swift:415-433`). This feature's scope and validation contract distinguish diff inspection from live-only git mutations: `VAL-LANES-006` requires lane detail to let users inspect file changes and diffs, while `VAL-LANES-008` says only live-only git actions should disable when disconnected or syncing (`/Users/admin/.factory/missions/3d6b3fd2-ee5a-4122-83b2-d6dff0c07f04/validation-contract.md:25-35`). The diff screen itself already only gates editing via `canEditDiff` (`apps/ios/ADE/Views/Lanes/LaneDiffScreen.swift:84-100`), so blocking entry into the diff turns cached lane detail into a dead end during the exact offline/sync states this feature was meant to preserve.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance still says the foundation hard gate from `.factory/services.yaml` is iOS-only, but the checked-in `test` command still runs the unrelated red desktop suite before the simulator tests. That mismatch remains shared-state debt for future iOS-only workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/admin/.factory/missions/3d6b3fd2-ee5a-4122-83b2-d6dff0c07f04/AGENTS.md:55-56,64-67` says unrelated red desktop failures should not block iOS-only work, while `/Users/admin/Projects/ADE/.ade/worktrees/mobile-droid-attempt-bbdcd095/.factory/services.yaml:4` still defines `test` as `npm --prefix apps/desktop run test && xcodebuild test ...`.

summary: Fail: cached-state polish and live-action gating landed, but the commit wrongly gates diff inspection behind live connectivity, so disconnected/syncing lane detail no longer preserves the full benchmark triage flow promised by the foundation Lanes contract.
